headline: Sg Kim Kim Poisoning Costs Pasir Gudang RM1.2B
context: A 2025 University of Malaya study estimated the total economic cost of the 2019 Sg Kim Kim chemical dumping at RM1.2 billion including healthcare, lost productivity, and remediation.

cards.big: The government called Sg Kim Kim 'fully remediated' in 2021.
cards.sub: 6 years later, 34 families still report chronic respiratory conditions linked to the original exposure.

cards.big: Only 2 of 7 identified polluters were prosecuted.
cards.sub: Maximum fine under the Environmental Quality Act 1974 is RM500,000 — less than 0.05% of the damage.

cards.big: 111 illegal dumping sites identified in Pasir Gudang since 2019.
cards.sub: DOE has 47 enforcement officers for all of Johor — one per 404 registered factories.

cards.big: Healthcare costs for 6,000 affected residents exceeded RM89M.
cards.sub: No class-action mechanism exists in Malaysian law for environmental tort claims.

cards.big: When maximum penalties are less than dumping savings, the law incentivises pollution.
Malaysia needs an environmental liability regime where cleanup costs fall on polluters, not taxpayers. The EQA 1974 maximum fines belong to another era.
